reasoning.A:
DECISION: Pass  
REASONING: I’m certain my own Q1 answer is correct (7 × 8 is straightforward), and Q2–Q4 are almost surely simple arithmetic as in recent games, where we’ve seen only one mistake across many puzzles.  

In the last several games, someone else (usually B or C) has volunteered in Round 1 every time except once, and when I passed in similar situations (Games 6 and 7), C immediately volunteered with the full set of capsules. That makes the probability that *no one* volunteers this round fairly low.  

If I pass, my death risk is mainly from a random kill in the unlikely event that nobody volunteers: about 25% × P(no volunteer), which is probably well under 10%, likely closer to 2–5% based on recent behavior. If I volunteer, I personally take on the full risk that any of Q2–Q4 might be wrong, which is plausibly around or above that level.  

Verifying would cost 1 coin and still leave me exposed to the same random-kill risk this round, and given how likely an immediate volunteer from B or C is, that coin is unlikely to buy any real benefit. So passing best balances survival odds and resource preservation.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: My puzzle 13 + 48 is trivial and I’m fully confident in my sealed answer. Across recent games, all puzzles have been simple arithmetic, every verification has agreed with existing capsules, and every volunteer using the straightforward Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D combination has succeeded. That makes the chance any capsule is wrong appear very low. If I don’t volunteer, there’s a real risk nobody does and we face a random elimination, giving me a 1-in-4 death chance regardless of my own correctness. Verifying first would cost a coin and still leave me exposed to that same random-death risk this round. Volunteering now avoids random elimination, keeps all 4 of my coins if the password is correct, and the probability the password is wrong seems much smaller than the 25% random-death risk.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I know my own answer for Q3 (6 × 11) is correct. All recent games have been simple arithmetic, and aside from one early mistake by D, every capsule has been correct on the first try, even without verification. There are no conflicting capsules now, so each of the others (Q1-A, Q2-B, Q4-D) is very likely right.  

If I don’t volunteer, I still face the random-death risk this round if nobody else steps up, and verifying doesn’t reduce that risk—it only costs a coin. Given the strong accuracy track record and the simplicity of the puzzles, the chance that this full password is correct is higher than my effective risk from possible random elimination, so volunteering with these four capsules maximizes my odds of surviving and keeping all 4 coins.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:
✅ B entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D
🎉 Everyone escapes! 🎉
